name: Command and Scripting Interpreter
description: Adversaries abuse command and script interpreters such as PowerShell, cmd.exe, or bash to execute commands, scripts, or binaries.

name: OS Credential Dumping
description: Adversaries dump credentials from operating system memory or storage, for example by reading LSASS memory to obtain account hashes and passwords.

name: Brute Force
description: Adversaries use brute force or password spraying techniques to guess valid account credentials against a service.

name: Remote Services
description: Adversaries use valid accounts to log into remote services such as SMB/PsExec, WinRM, or RDP to move laterally through a network.

name: Application Layer Protocol
description: Adversaries communicate with compromised hosts over application layer protocols to blend in with normal traffic, including HTTP/S beaconing frameworks.

name: Obfuscated Files or Information
description: Adversaries encode, encrypt, or otherwise obfuscate scripts and payloads to hinder detection and analysis.

name: Process Injection
description: Adversaries inject code into the address space of another process to evade process-based defenses and elevate privileges.

name: Network Service Discovery
description: Adversaries enumerate services running on remote hosts to identify targets for further exploitation.

name: Active Scanning
description: Adversaries scan networks for open ports, live hosts, and running services prior to exploitation.

name: Scheduled Task/Job
description: Adversaries create scheduled tasks or cron jobs to establish persistence and re-execute malicious code.

name: Valid Accounts
description: Adversaries use compromised or default valid accounts to maintain access and blend in with legitimate activity.

name: Exfiltration Over Web Service
description: Adversaries exfiltrate data to a legitimate external web service such as a paste site or cloud storage to blend in with normal traffic.
